rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks for component ports must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Implementation-level bindings and signal routing should be defined in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections are declared.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should relate visible inputs to visible outputs without referencing implementation-only subcomponent paths.
AGREE annex blocks must preserve AADL architecture scope and owner relationships when inserted into target components.
Avoid using AADL property names directly as AGREE variables; instead, expose them through typed ports or constants.
Ensure that all visible identifiers in AGREE annexes are properly scoped to the component type or implementation where they are used.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees should express relationships between visible features in component type annexes.
Implementation-level annex content must follow subcomponent and connection declarations in the implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific signal routing or bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless they are represented through variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
